challenges.task: Construct a candidate-independent source admitted by the published scope language but not faithfully handled by the claimed result, or prove the attempted construction inadmissible.
Show that two commitment-distinct sources can receive the same canonical preservation record, or document why the registered commitments separate them.
Find a pair classified as commitment-equivalent despite differing ontology, hidden state, admissibility, dependency, history, or termination commitments.
Identify an interpreter, adapter, decoder, invariant, canonicalizer, oracle, or semantic bridge omitted from a candidate ledger.
Determine whether candidate definitions, helper allowances, or benchmark construction privilege FARA or LTS-PROV; provide a concrete biased rule or reject the attack.
Construct an allegedly admissible representation change that alters commitments or an excluded change that is genuinely commitment-preserving.
Produce a strictly cheaper non-equivalent reconstruction after removing a load-bearing unit, or establish that all repairs restore commitment-equivalent machinery.
Challenge a dominance edge, translation-equivalence judgment, or frontier exclusion using the frozen componentwise preorder.
Locate a failed, boundary, negative-control, or unresolved result omitted or weakened in the terminal synthesis.
Identify any passage that upgrades bounded subclass, local necessity, frozen-universe minimality, or internal robustness into a global or externally validated claim.
Sketch at least one materially new non-FARA/non-LTS-PROV architecture and state which current conclusions it could alter; evaluation belongs to EVC-W4, but omission risk must be recorded here.
Find any inference from formal fit, generated traces, or label agreement to identity with an independently existing reasoning process.

completion_rule: All twelve challenges require an attempted construction, evidence references, and a disposition of sustained, rejected, or unresolved.
prohibited_shortcuts: treating inability to find a counterexample as proof of universality
using author assertions as the only rejection evidence
combining scope limitation with full refutation without justification
counting internal red-team simulation as R4 independence
